decisions.value: domain-blind primitives only; no Padeye/Shackle types
DXF + STL writers, with geom + scene + write/ layers
vendor pure-Python mapbox-earcut at cad/_vendor/earcut.py, MIT, NOTICE attribution
scene-level on DxfDrawing (add_text in Stage 1; add_dimension placeholder for Stage 3), never on geom
not on geom; deferred to STEP spec; Stage 1 plate-with-hole uses earcut polygon-with-holes, not boolean
intent not B-rep: Extrusion/Revolution are descriptions; each writer interprets (STL tessellates, future STEP builds B-rep)
SI metres; DXF $INSUNITS=6
R2018 (AC1032); no R12 fallback in Stage 1
binary by default; ascii=True flag for tests
Z-up, right-handed
3.11+; frozen slots dataclasses
8 significant figures in DXF/STL ASCII output
pure-stdlib; ezdxf is dev-only test dep
Tier1 ValueError in geom __post_init__; Tier2 SceneError at scene.add(); Tier3 WriteError during serialise; all share CadError base except Tier1
write-only; no DXF/STL parser, no round-trip
every geom type exposes bounds(): 2D returns (Vec2,Vec2), 3D returns (Vec3,Vec3)
tessellate.py contract includes Revolution: profile-points x angular-samples per tolerance, stitched into triangle bands
Layer.linetype defaults CONTINUOUS in Stage 1; HIDDEN/CENTER deferred to Stage 2

lowercase factory functions (line/arc/circle/rectangle/polyline/spline/sphere/prism); methods on shapes for transforms; .extrude/.revolve as methods on closed Shape2D bridge to Shape3D; per-format scenes (DxfDrawing, StlMesh)
Shape2D and Shape3D are disjoint ABCs sharing no methods; 2D translate(dx,dy) vs 3D translate(dx,dy,dz) are arity-different and statically checked
holes are a 2D-only concept via .with_hole/.with_holes methods on closed Shape2D; baked into profile before extrude; no Stage 1 boolean op on Shape3D
method-style: shape.translate(dx,dy[,dz]), shape.rotate(...), shape.mirror(...) — not free functions; geom values immutable, methods return new instances
+ composes adjacent 2D segments into a Path (head-to-tail, non-commutative); endpoints must match or ValueError; close() marks closed and auto-adds a closing line if needed
Vec2/Vec3 are internal value types; user code uses tuples (x,y) and (x,y,z); factory functions promote tuples to Vec*
axis arg accepts string in {+x,-x,+y,-y,+z,-z} OR Vec3; strings are 99% case; Vec3 is escape hatch
Layer.add() returns the layer for chaining; StlMesh.add() variadic and returns self for chaining
any 3D boolean attempt (solid - solid, solid.cut(...), etc.) raises TypeError in Stage 1 with message pointing at Stage 6 spec
SUPERSEDED — see transform-style and type-separation; 2D arity != 3D arity enforces correctness without overloads
SUPERSEDED — replaced by lowercase factory functions: rectangle((x,y),(w,h)), circle((x,y),r), polyline([pts])
Vec2/Vec3 internal arithmetic kept (+,-,scalar*,length,normalised,dot,cross); not exposed in user API surface — see vec-types-internal
Stage 2 Model is a thin facade over Stage 1 DxfDrawing and StlMesh scenes; writers are not refactored into a shared document abstraction
Model.write_dxf flattens all named Drawing2D containers into one DXF modelspace in insertion order
Model.write_stl aggregates all Part solids into one StlMesh; STL remains a triangle soup without part names
Assembly is metadata-only in Stage 2; DXF and STL exporters ignore assemblies until STEP/product structure work
Model.write_step exists but raises NotImplementedError until Stage 5
Stage 3 supports built-in CONTINUOUS, HIDDEN, and CENTER linetypes through Layer.linetype; custom linetype registration is deferred
HATCH is layer-level via Layer.hatch and ModelLayer.hatch; Stage 3 supports ANSI31 with a single closed flattened boundary
BLOCK definitions are drawing-local via DxfDrawing.block and Drawing2D.block; nested blocks and attributes are deferred
INSERT references are drawing-level via DxfDrawing.insert and Drawing2D.insert; insertion layer controls the INSERT entity layer
Model.write_dxf preserves hatches, linetypes, block definitions, and inserts from Drawing2D scenes; duplicate block names across drawings raise SceneError
Stage 4 dimensions are self-rendered as LINE and MTEXT primitives, not native DXF DIMENSION entities
HATCH supports one-level profile holes/islands through Shape2D.inner_loops
cad.geom.helpers exposes midpoint, perpendicular, and offset_point for drawing helper placement

deferred.item: Native DXF DIMENSION entities — deferred until editable CAD dimensions are worth the compatibility risk
Angular dimensions — deferred beyond Stage 4; linear/aligned/radius/diameter cover the production plate example
STEP writer (AP242 baseline, AP203 fallback) — Stage 5+; separate spec
Boolean cut for STEP — Stage 6; emit-two-solids vs CSG-on-mesh decided then
Fillets, blends, swept profiles, NURBS surfaces — explicit out-of-scope
Sheet templates and title blocks — recipe-level, not core; live in pyseas-yard or examples/
DXF version downgrade to R12 (no LWPOLYLINE) — only revisit if customer needs it
GD&T tolerance bands in STEP — out of scope for v1

TRIM operation — Stage 4 spec; given a coordinate near intersecting entities, cut at intersections and remove the closest sub-entity. Requires pairwise intersection algorithms (line/line, line/arc, line/circle, etc.) plus split_at and closest-entity search. Estimated ~2-3 weeks.

Sweep type — Stage 5+ alongside STEP work; sweep a 2D cross-section along a 3D path. Required for shackle bodies, U-bolts, pipe elbows. Currently no path equivalent of Polyline in 3D — would need Polyline3D or equivalent.

split_at(t) method on Line/Arc/Circle/Spline — Stage 1.5 or alongside TRIM in Stage 4; cut a 2D segment at parameter t in [0,1] returning two sub-entities. Cheapest precursor for trim/edit operations. Standalone useful for recipes that need to break long curves into pieces.

intersect(a, b) function for 2D entity pairs — Stage 4 alongside TRIM. Pairwise: line/line, line/arc, line/circle minimum; full matrix is ~10 algorithms. Returns 0..N intersection points. Required by TRIM but standalone useful.
Path.simplify() — Stage 1.5; auto-merge adjacent path segments that share endpoints (e.g., two collinear lines into one). Helps recipes built from many small pieces.
Advanced geometry helpers: tangent_circle(c1,c2,r), offset_curve(curve, distance), split/intersect helpers — deferred beyond the Stage 4 minimum
3D fillet/chamfer/blend operations on extruded edges — Stage 6+ alongside boolean kernel. 2D chamfers are workable today by building 45-degree segments into the outline.
Sheet type hosting multiple 2D views — out of scope for pyseas-cad; layout belongs in pyseas-yard or a separate sheet-composition package.
